content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer CCC. Let ddd be the smallest integer such that 2d2^d2d is strictly greater than CCC.

Consider all pairs of non-negative integers (A,B)(A,B)(A,B) such that A,B<2dA,B \lt 2^dA,B<2d and A⊕B=CA \oplus B = CA⊕B=C (⊕\oplus⊕ denotes the bitwise XOR operation). Find the maximum value of A⋅BA \cdot BA⋅B over all these pairs.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer CCC.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the maximum possible product A⋅BA \cdot BA⋅B.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The binary representation of 131313 is "1101". We can use A=10A = 10A=10 ("1010" in binary) and B=7B = 7B=7 ("0111" in binary). This gives us the product 707070. No other valid pair (A,B)(A, B)(A,B) can give us a larger product.
Example case 2: The binary representation of 101010 is "1010". We can use A=13A = 13A=13 ("1101") and B=7B = 7B=7 ("0111"). This gives us the maximum product 919191.
